violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer taken out of service until properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026
Various foods were observed held past a day or date that exceeds 7 days. Product disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Dishwasher in kitchen to not be used until serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of bio-film. Ice machine turned off and to be cleaned and sanitized once empty. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at with RIDOH. Per PIC, application has been filled out and given to boss. Establishment to submit application to RIDOH. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Only cook/manager at night is certified currently. Person to be certified at all times. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

All food in walk-in was observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Unit was adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Bottles were labeled at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
Knives were observed stored between prep units. Knives were brought to dish machine to be sanitized. Knives shall be stored on sanitary surfaces. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
The walk-in was observed at 43 degrees. Unit was observed very dusty in back. Unit to be cleaned and was adjusted at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving above the mixer have an accumulation of dust and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. One person certified in food safety needs to be present at all times. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024

Knives were observed stuck between prep units and prep tables. All knives were removed and brought to dishwasher. Proper utensils storage reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024
